## Summary

Describe the change in a few short paragraphs.

Include:

- what changed
- why it changed
- the issue or task this PR addresses
- any important scope boundaries or non-goals

## Linked Issue

- Closes #

## Validation

List the checks you ran and what they covered.

Examples:

- `make test`
- `make lint`
- `make verify`
- `make ci`

Also note anything you could not validate locally.

## Agent Execution Context

Select one:

- [ ] Agent-authored
- [ ] Agent-assisted
- [ ] Developer-authored without agent assistance

If an agent was involved, include:

- agent/runner used:
- supervising developer:
- how the agent was used:
- anything the reviewer should pay particular attention to:

Because this repository typically uses squash and merge, this section is the primary durable record of agent involvement for the change.

## Change Type

Select all that apply:

- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Bug fix
- [ ] Refactor
- [ ] Documentation
- [ ] Test-only change
- [ ] Build / CI / tooling
- [ ] Dependency change

## Impact Review

Select any areas affected by this PR:

- [ ] Architecture or application boundaries
- [ ] Security, authentication, authorisation, or access control
- [ ] Persistence, migrations, or data shape
- [ ] External APIs or integrations
- [ ] Local development workflow
- [ ] CI, release, or deployment workflow
- [ ] Performance-sensitive paths
- [ ] None of the above

If any of the above are checked, summarise the impact briefly.

## Risks and Follow-ups

List:

- known risks
- assumptions made
- deferred work
- follow-up issues or cleanups that should happen later

## Reviewer Notes

Call out anything that would help a reviewer assess this PR efficiently.

Examples:

- files or modules to review first
- trade-offs made
- areas of uncertainty
- places where acceptance criteria were interpreted narrowly
